title: JavaScript Concepts
response:
Arrays store ordered lists of values. JavaScript arrays are dynamic, can hold mixed types, and have a rich set of built-in methods.

Must-know array methods:
• map(fn) — transforms every element, returns new array.
• filter(fn) — keeps elements where fn returns true.
• reduce(fn, init) — accumulates a single value.
• forEach(fn) — iterates, no return value.
• find(fn) — returns first matching element.
• findIndex(fn) — returns index of first match.
• some(fn) — true if at least one element matches.
• every(fn) — true if all elements match.
• flat(depth) — flattens nested arrays.
• flatMap(fn) — map then flatten one level.
• includes(val) — checks if value exists.
• splice / slice — mutate / copy portions.
• sort(fn) — sorts in place (mutates!).
